mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal deteriorating labor income and corporate revenue outlook, which forward-looking equity markets price in as reduced earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137640 thousand reflecting ongoing 2008 employment contraction
failure_condition: If PAYEMS declines for 3+ consecutive months but SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of such instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below a critical threshold indicating a sustained residential construction collapse, the resulting drag on household wealth, construction employment, and credit markets propagates into broader economic contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1000 thousand units and remains below for 2+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 975 thousand units, below the 1000k threshold in July 2008
failure_condition: If periods with HOUST below 1000k show no statistically significant negative 60-day SPX return relative to baseline (t-test p > 0.05), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 4% year-over-year while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains historically low, the Fed is constrained from easing further to support growth, elevating equity risk premia and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as stagflation fears dominate.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 215.132 with elevated inflation pressure amid FEDFUNDS at 2.0% in mid-2008
failure_condition: If the joint condition (high CPI YoY, low FEDFUNDS) is triggered but SPX 20-day forward returns are positive on average or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling windows, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects contracting manufacturing output and reduced corporate earnings capacity, triggering a rapid negative repricing of equities within the following 5 trading days as markets react to the incoming data release.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 110.9051, showing signs of flattening/decline in mid-2008 industrial activity
failure_condition: If INDPRO MoM declines are not followed by negative SPX 5-day forward returns in a statistically significant majority of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A Sahm-rule-like threshold crossing, where the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises 0.3 percentage points or more above its trailing 3-month low, marks the onset of a labor market recession that historically precedes sustained equity market drawdowns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.3pp above its 3-month trailing low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.5% in mid-2008, up from cyclical lows near 4.4-4.7% in 2006-2007
regime_hint: labor market turning point / early recession onset
failure_condition: If triggered instances of the UNRATE threshold crossing do not show significantly negative 60-day SPX forward returns compared to non-triggered periods (p > 0.05), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion characterized by slowing consumer momentum, deceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) growth signals weakening final demand that has not yet been fully priced into equities, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as consumption data confirms the slowdown.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 384211 million dollars amid signs of consumer spending deceleration in mid-2008
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with slowing consumer demand
failure_condition: If RSAFS deceleration episodes in this regime do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05), or the effect reverses in out-of-sample periods, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains unchanged for an extended period while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) simultaneously rises, it signals monetary policy paralysis in which the Fed cannot ease due to inflation concerns despite deteriorating labor conditions, amplifying equity risk premia and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS unchanged for 3+ consecutive months AND UNRATE increases by >= 0.3pp over the same period
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS held near 2.0% since April 2008 while UNRATE rose to 5.5%
regime_hint: monetary policy paralysis amid stagflation risk
failure_condition: If instances of frozen FEDFUNDS combined with rising UNRATE do not show significantly negative 60-day forward SPX returns relative to baseline, or Rank IC < 0.03, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous multi-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a broad-based, housing-led industrial slowdown that triggers rapid risk-off equity repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the compounded weakness is confirmed across supply and demand-side data.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines for 3+ consecutive months AND INDPRO posts a month-over-month decline
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 975 thousand units and INDPRO at 110.9051 showing concurrent softness in mid-2008
failure_condition: If joint HOUST and INDPRO decline episodes fail to produce significantly negative 5-day SPX forward returns compared to single-signal baselines (p > 0.05), the hypothesis is rejected.
